epic.name: Escalation Policies UI & Visualization
epic.objective: Build the frontend for creating, managing, and visualizing escalation policies, including a flowchart-style policy viewer and integration with routing rules.
epic.definition_of_done: Users can create escalation policies with multiple tiers via the web UI, visualize escalation flow as a diagram, assign policies to routing rules, and see escalation status on the alert detail page.

design_decisions.policy_visualization: Vertical flowchart: Tier 0 (box with schedule/users, timeout) → Arrow → Tier 1 → ... → Repeat indicator
design_decisions.tier_target_selector: Dropdown: 'Schedule' | 'Users' | 'Both' → conditional input (schedule picker OR user multi-input)
design_decisions.timeout_input: Number input + unit dropdown (minutes/hours) for UX, converted to seconds on submit
design_decisions.policy_list_view: Table with columns: Name, Tiers (count), Enabled, Actions (edit/delete/duplicate)
design_decisions.routing_rule_integration: Add 'Escalation Policy' dropdown to routing rule actions form
design_decisions.alert_detail_escalation_status: Collapsible section: 'Escalation Timeline' showing tier notifications and acknowledgment
design_decisions.acknowledgment_button: Prominent 'Acknowledge Alert' button on alert detail page (calls POST /api/v1/alerts/:id/acknowledge)
design_decisions.user_input_format: Comma-separated user_name list (email or display name), no validation in v0.5

tasks.name: Escalation policies list and detail pages
tasks.description: List page for all escalation policies and detail page showing policy configuration
acceptance_criteria: GET /escalation-policies → EscalationPoliciesPage: table with name, tier count, enabled badge, actions
Empty state: 'No escalation policies yet' with CTA to create first policy
GET /escalation-policies/:id → EscalationPolicyDetailPage: policy metadata, tier list, flowchart visualization
Sidebar nav item: AlertTriangle icon, label 'Escalation', path /escalation-policies
Tier list: each tier shows tier_index, target_type, target details (schedule name or user_name list), timeout (human-readable)
Policy enabled toggle: PATCH /api/v1/escalation-policies/:id with { enabled: true/false }
Loading states and error handling
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow existing patterns (SchedulesPage, RoutingRulesPage). Add useEscalationPolicies and useEscalationPolicy hooks in api/escalation.ts. Flowchart visualization deferred to OI-132. Tier target details: if target_type='schedule', show schedule.name via join; if 'users', show comma-separated user_names.

tasks.name: Policy flowchart visualization
tasks.description: Interactive flowchart showing escalation policy tiers as a vertical flow diagram
acceptance_criteria: Flowchart component on EscalationPolicyDetailPage: vertical boxes connected by arrows
Each tier box shows: tier index, target (schedule/users), timeout duration
Arrow labels indicate 'If unacknowledged after [timeout]'
Final tier has 'Repeat from Tier 0' indicator (or 'Stop' if no-repeat configured)
Click tier box → opens edit tier modal (deferred to OI-133)
Responsive: vertical flow on all screen sizes (no horizontal scroll needed)
Color-coded boxes: tier 0 (blue), tier 1 (orange), tier 2+ (gray)
tasks.implementation_notes: Build custom flowchart component using SVG or HTML+CSS (no external flowchart library). Use flexbox column layout with arrows as pseudo-elements or SVG paths. Each tier is a card with icon (Schedule: Calendar, Users: User icon), target name, and timeout badge. Future enhancement: drag-to-reorder tiers (deferred to v0.6).

tasks.name: Escalation policy create/edit UI
tasks.description: Forms for creating policies and managing tiers
acceptance_criteria: Create Policy button opens modal: name (required), description (optional), enabled checkbox (default true)
Created policy navigates to detail page with 'Add Tier' panel
Add Tier panel: tier_index (auto-incremented), timeout (number + minutes/hours dropdown), target_type (dropdown: Schedule/Users/Both)
Conditional inputs: if 'Schedule', show schedule dropdown (fetched from GET /api/v1/schedules); if 'Users', show text input (comma-separated); if 'Both', show both
Validation: timeout > 0, target_type required, appropriate target selected
Edit tier: click tier in flowchart → opens modal pre-filled with tier data → PATCH updates tier
Delete tier: trash icon on tier card → window.confirm → DELETE /api/v1/escalation-policies/:id/tiers/:tier_id → refetch policy
Delete policy: delete button with warning if policy is assigned to routing rules
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow inline modal pattern. Timeout: default unit 'minutes', convert to seconds (minutes * 60, hours * 3600). Schedule dropdown: useSchedules hook, map to { value: schedule.id, label: schedule.name }. User input: text input, split by comma on submit, trim whitespace. Edit tier: PATCH /api/v1/escalation-policies/:id/tiers/:tier_id with updated fields.

tasks.name: Routing rules integration for escalation policies
tasks.description: Extend routing rules UI to allow assigning escalation policies to routing actions
acceptance_criteria: Routing rule actions form: add 'Escalation Policy' field (dropdown, optional)
Dropdown options: fetch from GET /api/v1/escalation-policies, show policy name
When routing rule matches alert, if escalation_policy_id is set, backend triggers escalation (Epic 018 handles logic)
Routing rule table: show escalation policy name in 'Actions' column summary (e.g., 'Escalate: Default Escalation')
Edit routing rule: pre-populate escalation policy dropdown with existing value
Validation: escalation policy must exist (frontend validates via fetched list)
tasks.implementation_notes: Modify RoutingRulesPage RuleModal component. Add escalation_policy_id to actions JSONB in RoutingRule model (backend already supports arbitrary actions fields). Frontend: useEscalationPolicies hook fetches policies for dropdown. Actions summary: if actions.escalation_policy_id, fetch policy name and display 'Escalate: {name}'.

tasks.name: Alert acknowledgment UI
tasks.description: Alert detail page showing escalation status and acknowledgment button
acceptance_criteria: Alert detail page: add 'Escalation Status' collapsible section (collapsed by default)
Escalation status shows: policy name, current tier, time since last notification, tier timeline (tier 0 notified at X, tier 1 notified at Y)
If unacknowledged: prominent 'Acknowledge Alert' button → POST /api/v1/alerts/:id/acknowledge → refetch alert
If acknowledged: show 'Acknowledged by [user_name] at [timestamp] via [slack/api]' badge (green, checkmark icon)
If escalation not triggered (no policy): section hidden or shows 'No escalation policy assigned'
Acknowledgment confirmation toast: 'Alert acknowledged successfully'
Error handling: if acknowledge fails, show error toast with API message
tasks.implementation_notes: Extend GET /api/v1/alerts/:id response to include escalation_state (policy_id, current_tier_index, last_notified_at, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_via). Frontend: AlertDetailPage component (create if doesn't exist). Acknowledgment button: POST with { user_name: 'web-user', acknowledged_via: 'api' } (user_name hardcoded in v0.5, replaced with auth.user in v0.9).

tasks.name: Escalation policies API client and types
tasks.description: Frontend API client for escalation policies CRUD operations and TypeScript types
acceptance_criteria: frontend/src/api/escalation.ts: listEscalationPolicies, getEscalationPolicy, createEscalationPolicy, updateEscalationPolicy, deleteEscalationPolicy
frontend/src/api/escalation.ts: createTier, updateTier, deleteTier
TypeScript types: EscalationPolicy, EscalationTier, EscalationState (matching backend DTOs)
useEscalationPolicies and useEscalationPolicy hooks using react-query
acknowledgeAlert function in api/alerts.ts: POST /api/v1/alerts/:id/acknowledge
Error handling and loading states for all API calls
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow existing api/ patterns. Types: export interface EscalationPolicy { id: string; name: string; description: string; enabled: boolean; tiers: EscalationTier[]; created_at: string; updated_at: string }. Tier: { id: string; policy_id: string; tier_index: number; timeout_seconds: number; target_type: 'schedule'|'users'|'both'; schedule_id?: string; user_ids?: string[]; }. State: { id, alert_id, policy_id, current_tier_index, last_notified_at, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_via }.

deferred_items.reason: Tiers can be created in correct order; reordering is nice-to-have

deferred_items.item: Slack user picker for user targets
deferred_items.reason: Free-text comma-separated input works for v0.5; picker requires Slack API integration
Default repeat behavior covers 95% of use cases
